---
title: "ggpaintr Showcase Demo"
author: "Will Ju"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, eval = FALSE)
```

## About this demo

`ggpaintr` turns a ggplot-like formula string into a Shiny app. Placeholder
tokens inside the formula (`var`, `text`, `num`, `expr`, `upload`) become
Shiny widgets automatically.

Run chunks one at a time. Each `ptr_app*()` call launches an interactive app;
close the window before launching the next one.

```{r packages}
library(ggpaintr)
library(ggplot2)
library(shiny)
library(shinyWidgets)
library(rlang)
# Install once if missing: install.packages("colourpicker")
library(colourpicker)
```

---

# Part 1 — Core Features

## Example 1.1: The simplest possible app

One formula, two `var` pickers, one `text` box. Everything else is free.

```{r basic}
ptr_app("
ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = text)
")
```

Try: `x = Sepal.Length`, `y = Sepal.Width`, `title = Iris scatter`, then
click **Update plot**. Watch the generated code pane mirror your choices.

## Example 1.2: All five placeholders in one app

`var` (column picker), `text` (string), `num` (numeric), `expr` (parsed R
expression), `upload` (file input). Each layer is toggleable.

```{r all-placeholders}
ptr_app("
ggplot(data = upload, aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = var), size = num, alpha = num) +
  geom_smooth(method = text, se = expr) +
  labs(title = text, x = text, y = text) +
  facet_wrap(expr) +
  theme(legend.position = text)
")
```

Suggested flow:

- upload `tests/testthat/fixtures/simple_numeric.csv` (blank the dataset name)
- `x = x`, `y = y`, `color = group`
- `size = 2.5`, `alpha = 0.7`
- `method = lm`, `se = TRUE` (expr, parsed as R)
- `title = Uploaded scatter`, `legend.position = bottom`
- `facet_wrap expr = ~ group`

Uncheck individual layers to see them vanish from both plot and code.

## Example 1.3: `expr` as a compatibility escape hatch

`expr` is parsed as an R expression at draw time, so any ggplot2 feature
(scales, coords, stats, annotations, vector-valued args) can be dropped in
without needing a dedicated placeholder.

```{r expr-compat}
ptr_app("
ggplot(data = mtcars, aes(x = var, y = var, color = factor(cyl))) +
  geom_point(size = num) +
  geom_smooth(method = text, formula = expr, se = expr) +
  scale_color_manual(values = expr) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = expr, ylim = expr) +
  labs(title = text) +
  expr
")
```

Suggested inputs showing the full reach of `expr`:

- `x = wt`, `y = mpg`, `size = 3`
- `method = lm`, `formula = y ~ x`, `se = FALSE`
- `scale_color_manual values = c('4' = 'steelblue', '6' = 'firebrick', '8' = 'goldenrod')`
- `coord_cartesian xlim = c(1, 6)`, `ylim = c(10, 35)`
- `title = mtcars with expr features`
- Trailing `expr = theme_minimal(base_size = 14) + theme(legend.position = 'top')`

Any ggplot component you can write in R code, you can pipe through `expr`.

---

# Part 2 — Three Levels of Extensibility

## Level 1: One-liner wrapper with UI text overrides

`ptr_app()` / `ptr_app_bslib()` — the shipped wrappers. Customize visible
copy with `ui_text` without writing any Shiny code.

```{r extensibility-level-1}
ptr_app_bslib(
  "
ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = var, y = var)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = var), size = num) +
  labs(title = text)
",
ui_text = list(
  shell = list(
    title       = list(label = "Iris Explorer"),
    draw_button = list(label = "Render")
  ),
  params = list(
    x     = list(var  = list(label = "X-axis column")),
    y     = list(var  = list(label = "Y-axis column")),
    color = list(var  = list(label = "Colour by")),
    title = list(text = list(label = "Chart title"))
  ),
  layers = list(
    geom_point = list(num = list(size = list(
      label = "Point size",
      help  = "Typical values: 1 – 5"
    )))
  )
)
)
```

## Level 2: Embed into your own Shiny layout

`ptr_server_state()` plus the `ptr_register_*()` bind helpers let you drop
`ggpaintr` into a larger Shiny UI with custom ids. You own the layout; the
helpers wire up the runtime.

```{r extensibility-level-2}
custom_ids <- ptr_build_ids(
  control_panel = "builder_controls",
  draw_button   = "render_plot",
  plot_output   = "main_plot",
  error_output  = "main_error",
  code_output   = "main_code"
)

ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("Embedded ggpaintr"),
  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      ptr_input_ui(ids = custom_ids),
      tags$hr(),
      h4("Session status"),
      textOutput("runtimeStatus")
    ),
    mainPanel(
      ptr_output_ui(ids = custom_ids)
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  ptr_state <- ptr_server_state(
    "ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = var, y = var)) +
      geom_point(aes(color = var), size = num) +
      labs(title = text)",
    ids = custom_ids
  )
  
  ptr_setup_controls(input, output, ptr_state, ids = custom_ids)
  ptr_register_draw(input, ptr_state, ids = custom_ids)
  ptr_register_plot(output, ptr_state, ids = custom_ids)
  ptr_register_error(output, ptr_state, ids = custom_ids)
  ptr_register_code(output, ptr_state, ids = custom_ids)
  
  # Your own reactive reading ptr_state$runtime()
  output$runtimeStatus <- renderText({
    r <- ptr_state$runtime()
    if (is.null(r))          return("Waiting for first draw...")
    if (isTRUE(r$ok))        return(paste("OK —", nchar(r$code_text), "chars of code"))
    r$message
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
```

## Level 3: Own the render path with pure extractors + `ptr_gg_extra()`

Swap in your own `renderPlot()`. Pull the built plot with `ptr_extract_plot()`,
append any ggplot components, and wrap those components with
`ptr_gg_extra()` so they round-trip into the generated-code pane too.

```{r extensibility-level-3}
ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("Custom render path"),
  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(ptr_input_ui()),
    mainPanel(
      plotOutput("outputPlot", height = "500px"),
      uiOutput("outputError"),
      verbatimTextOutput("outputCode")
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  ptr_state <- ptr_server_state(
    "ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = var, y = var, color = Species)) +
      geom_point(size = num) +
      labs(title = text)"
  )
  
  ptr_setup_controls(input, output, ptr_state)
  ptr_register_draw(input, ptr_state)
  ptr_register_error(output, ptr_state)
  ptr_register_code(output, ptr_state)
  
  output$outputPlot <- renderPlot({
    plot_obj <- ptr_extract_plot(ptr_state$runtime())
    if (is.null(plot_obj)) {
      plot.new(); return(invisible(NULL))
    }
    
    # ptr_gg_extra() captures these additions so ptr_register_code()
    # appends them to outputCode — keeps the rendered plot and the
    # code pane in sync.
    plot_obj + ptr_gg_extra(
      ptr_state,
      theme_minimal(base_size = 16),
      scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2"),
      theme(legend.position = "bottom")
    )
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
```

---

# Part 3 — Custom Placeholders

## Example 3.1: A `color` placeholder with `colourpicker::colourInput`

Replace the default `text`-box approach for colours with a real colour
picker. `copy_defaults` ships labels; `resolve_expr()` quotes the chosen
colour as a string literal in generated code.

```{r color-placeholder}
# install.packages("colourpicker")  # if needed

color_placeholder <- ptr_define_placeholder(
  keyword = "color",
  
  # build_ui: one widget per placeholder occurrence in the formula.
  build_ui = function(id, copy, meta, context) {
    default <- if (identical(meta$param, "fill")) "#4E79A7" else "#E15759"
    colourpicker::colourInput(
      id,
      label = copy$label,
      value = default,
      # showColour = "both",
      # palette = "limited"
    )
  },
  
  # resolve_expr: chosen colour → a quoted string literal in the code.
  resolve_expr = function(value, meta, context) {
    if (is.null(value) || identical(value, "")) {
      return(ptr_missing_expr())
    }
    rlang::expr(!!as.character(value))
  },
  
  copy_defaults = list(label = "Pick {param} colour")
)

color_placeholders <- ptr_merge_placeholders(
  list(color = color_placeholder)
)

ptr_app(
  "
ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = Sepal.Length, y = Sepal.Width)) +
  geom_point(color = color, fill = color, size = num, shape = 21) +
  geom_smooth(method = 'lm', color = color, se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = text)
",
placeholders = color_placeholders
)
```

Try picking three different colours and watch the generated code update with
the hex strings.

## Example 3.2: A custom `date` placeholder with param-aware copy

A second custom placeholder: a `dateInput` whose chosen date is spliced
as `as.Date("YYYY-MM-DD")` into the generated code. Shows how
`ptr_merge_ui_text()` layers copy rules for a custom keyword.

```{r date-placeholder}
sales <- data.frame(
  day     = as.Date("2024-01-01") + 0:9,
  value   = c(10, 13, 12, 16, 18, 17, 19, 22, 20, 24),
  segment = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 5)
)

date_placeholder <- ptr_define_placeholder(
  keyword = "date",
  build_ui = function(id, copy, meta, context) {
    shiny::dateInput(id, label = copy$label, value = "2024-01-01")
  },
  resolve_expr = function(value, meta, context) {
    if (is.null(value) || identical(as.character(value), "")) {
      return(ptr_missing_expr())
    }
    rlang::expr(as.Date(!!as.character(value)))
  },
  copy_defaults = list(label = "Choose a date for {param}")
)

date_placeholders <- ptr_merge_placeholders(list(date = date_placeholder))

date_ui_text <- ptr_merge_ui_text(
  list(
    defaults = list(date = list(label = "Pick any date")),
    params   = list(xintercept = list(date = list(label = "Reference date"))),
    layers   = list(
      geom_vline = list(date = list(xintercept = list(
        help = "Cut-off date for the vertical guide."
      )))
    )
  ),
  placeholders = date_placeholders
)

ptr_app(
  "
ggplot(data = sales, aes(x = day, y = value, color = segment)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(size = num) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = date, linetype = 'dashed') +
  labs(title = text)
",
placeholders = date_placeholders,
ui_text      = date_ui_text
)
```

Change the reference date; the vertical line and the `as.Date(...)` string in
the code pane both update on draw.


```{r}
library(ggiraph)

ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("Custom render path"),
  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(ptr_input_ui()),
    mainPanel(
      # plotOutput("outputPlot", height = "500px"),
      ggiraph::girafeOutput("outputPlot"),
      uiOutput("outputError"),
      verbatimTextOutput("outputCode")
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  ptr_state <- ptr_server_state(
    "ggplot(data = iris, aes(x = var, y = var, color = Species)) +
      geom_point_interactive(
        aes( data_id = var, tooltip = var), size = 3,
        hover_nearest = TRUE) +
      labs(title = text)"
  )
  
  ptr_setup_controls(input, output, ptr_state)
  ptr_register_draw(input, ptr_state)
  ptr_register_error(output, ptr_state)
  ptr_register_code(output, ptr_state)
  
  output$outputPlot <- renderGirafe({
    plot_obj <- ptr_extract_plot(ptr_state$runtime())
    if (is.null(plot_obj)) {
      plot.new(); return(invisible(NULL))
    }
    
    x <- girafe(code = print(plot_obj),
                width_svg = 6, height_svg = 5,
                options = list(
                  opts_hover(css = "fill:#FF3333;stroke:black;cursor:pointer;", reactive = TRUE),
                  opts_selection(
                    type = "multiple", css = "fill:#FF3333;stroke:black;")
                ))
    x
    
    # ptr_gg_extra() captures these additions so ptr_register_code()
    # appends them to outputCode — keeps the rendered plot and the
    # code pane in sync.
    # plot_obj + ptr_gg_extra(
    #   ptr_state,
    #   theme_minimal(base_size = 16),
    #   scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2"),
    #   theme(legend.position = "bottom")
    # )
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)

```


---

# Features not covered by this demo

Everything below the line is supported but intentionally left out of this
showcase. Flag them if the audience asks "can it…?"

**Placeholder hook surface (advanced)**

- `resolve_input()` — preprocessing a raw Shiny value before `resolve_expr()`
sees it (e.g. `trimws()`). Example 3.x only used the default.
- `bind_ui()` — deferred / reactive UI registration. This is how the built-in
`var` placeholder populates column choices *after* an upload resolves. Not
shown here because the demo placeholders are static.
- `prepare_eval_env()` — injecting arbitrary R objects into the eval
environment right before the plot is built. This is how the built-in
`upload` placeholder makes an uploaded data frame visible by name.

**Formula / runtime mechanics**

- `ptr_parse_formula()` + `ptr_runtime_input_spec()` + `ptr_exec()` — the
low-level programmatic path for tests, tooling, or package extensions
around a parsed formula (no Shiny session required).
- `ptr_normalize_column_names()` + the full local-object / non-syntactic
column workflow (Formula 2C in the manual workbook).
- Mixed **global + layer-specific** uploads in one formula (two independent
upload widgets, two independent `var` pools).
- Layer toggles and the "uncheck every optional layer still yields a valid
base `ggplot(...)`" graceful-degradation path.
- Formula-level `var` transforms like `aes(x = log(var), y = var + 1)`.

**Error feedback**

- Inline `Input error:` vs `Plot error:` channels, plot-clears-on-failure,
code-survives-completion behaviour, recovery after a draw fails.

**UI text system**

- `ptr_resolve_ui_text()` introspection, `empty_text` / `placeholder` fields,
`__unnamed__` positional-arg copy, full precedence chain
(`layers > params > defaults > copy_defaults`).

**App shell**

- `ptr_app_bslib()` bslib theming beyond the single usage in Level 1.
- Using `ptr_extract_error()` / `ptr_extract_code()` to build a bespoke error
/ code pane (only `ptr_extract_plot()` was shown).

If any of these would help the showcase, say which and I'll wire up a
targeted example.
